task_completion.description: Adjust the CT window/level settings to appropriately view lung tissue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CT scan is visible in the viewer with a 'lung window' setting applied (typically W: ~1500, L: ~-600), making the dark lung parenchyma and brighter nodules clearly visible rather than washed out or completely black.

task_completion.description: Place fiducial markers on detected lung nodules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Crosshairs, colored points, or fiducial markers are visibly placed over lung nodules on the CT scan slices in the medical imaging viewer.

task_completion.description: Measure the longest diameter of the detected nodules to ensure they are >=3mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Measurement tools (such as a ruler or line tool) are visible on the CT scan, drawn across the nodules, displaying measurement values in millimeters.

task_completion.description: Determine and note the lobe location for the detected nodules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text annotations in the viewer, GUI panels, or draft notes in a text editor visible on screen indicating lobe names (RUL, RML, RLL, LUL, LLL) associated with the specific nodules.
A save dialog, terminal command, or file explorer showing the creation or saving of a file named 'agent_fiducials.fcsv' in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/' directory.

task_completion.description: Save the nodule report containing lobe locations and diameters to ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/nodule_report.json.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, IDE, or terminal showing a JSON structure containing lobe locations and diameter measurements, being saved to 'nodule_report.json' in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a medical imaging viewer (e.g., 3D Slicer) to view the CT scan and place markers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the UI of a medical imaging application with the CT scan loaded and interacted with, not just a terminal or code editor running a hidden script.

integrity.description: The agent actively measured the nodules using the software's tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ruler or caliper tools are actively drawn on the nodules in the viewer, proving the agent didn't just guess or hardcode the diameters.

integrity.description: The agent generated the JSON report based on its own findings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing, formatting, or running a script to compile its own fiducial and measurement data into the JSON format, rather than copying a pre-existing 'ground_truth' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The minimum nodule diameter to report is 3.0 mm. Nodules smaller than 3.0 mm should not be marked or included in the final report.
